criteria.name: Humor Integration (The Hummiston/Hormozi Standard)
criteria.description: Jokes must be 'load-bearing.' They should use the serious setup as the punchline rather than stopping the video to 'tell a joke.' Is it situational? Does the seriousness set it up? Does it end in an 'Abrupt Snap'?
criteria.scale: 5=Mocking the data, serious setup creates the snap; 3=Humor Gap (a temporary break from content); 1=Generic or dad jokes

criteria.name: Expression Economy & Brand 'Lore'
criteria.description: Prevent brand slogans from becoming repetitive filler. Ensure proprietary terms are defined before they are used as a mantra.
criteria.scale: 5=Used once at high impact, terms defined early; 3=Used 2-3 times losing punch, definition buried; 1=Relies on catchphrases as crutch, unexplained lore

criteria.name: Rhythmic Flow (The Rule of 3s)
criteria.description: The script should feel like a late-night strategy session between peers, not a keynote speech. Make frequent use of the Rule of Three for punchy delivery.
criteria.scale: 5=Natural progression, frequent Rule of 3s; 3=Distinct listicle-style sections; 1=Heavy Corporate Speak or overly formal transitions

criteria.name: Procedural vs. Declarative Knowledge
criteria.description: Move from philosophy to actionable decisions. Include concrete data points or operational changes.
criteria.scale: 5=Concrete 'Procedural Moment' with specific data; 3=Describes learning process but lacks Monday Morning application; 1=Purely philosophical

evaluation_prompt: You are a senior brand auditor for the 'Operational Realist' persona. Read the provided script and evaluate it against the following rubric. Provide a score for each criterion and a brief 1-sentence justification. Ensure your total score is the sum of the four criteria (Max 20).
